dispatch.return_message: Across all three rounds, the agent failed because of a compounding stack of NeuroKit2 API surface mismatches — wrong column name (`EDA_SCR_Peaks`) in round 1, correct computation but wrong condition labels (generic vs. Photosensor-encoded values) in round 2, and an internal crash inside `nk.bio_analyze`'s RSA sub-routine (`KeyError: 'RSA_P2T'`) in round 3 — meaning no single tactical patch could fix all three simultaneously, as each repair exposed a new, independent NeuroKit2 API pitfall.

record** that summarises a 3-round Self-Debug exploration of one
ScienceAgentBench task. All three rounds failed.

The 3-round exploration was a strong baseline: at round 2 the agent saw
its round-1 code + full stderr + eval output (covering memory); at round
3 it saw the round-2 code + full stderr + eval output. Despite this, all
three attempts failed. Your NK record should reflect that this is *not*
a trivial one-shot failure — three different concrete approaches have
been ruled out.

# Task

Task id: `029`

Task instruction:
> Localize the events from the biosignals of one participant in "bio_eventrelated_100hz.csv". Analyze the event-related features (e.g., ECG Rate, SCR Peak, Respiratory Volume per Time). Save the results to "pred_results/bio_eventrelated_100hz_analysis_pred.csv" with the following four columns: "Condition", "ECG_Rate_Mean", "RSP_Rate_Mean", and "EDA_Peak_Amplitude".

# Inputs you may read (and only these)

You will read up to 11 files. Do not read anything else.

**Round 1 (no memory, free attempt):**
1. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_029/primary_4.6/v3/round1/candidate.py` — round-1 script
2. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_029/primary_4.6/v3/round1/exec.log` — stdout / stderr / runtime errors

**Round 2 (Self-Debug covering memory of round 1):**

**Round 3 (Self-Debug covering memory of round 2):**

11. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_029/primary_4.6/v3/round3_B2/eval.log` — eval.log status for round 3: ABSENT — script crashed before producing output, evaluator not run.
12. `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/experiments/pilot_2026-05-10/runs/task_029/primary_4.6/v3/round3_B2/reasoning.md`

Read all available files before writing.

# Your output (exactly one Write call)

Write a JSON file via the Write tool to:

`/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/Negative_Knowledge/section3/04_outputs/nk_records/task_029_deep.json`

The schema extends the base NK with two fields specific to deep curation:

```json
{
  "task_id": "029",
  "depth": 3,
  "rounds_summary": [
    {
      "round": 1,
      "attempted_route": "<= 200 chars; what method/library/parameter was tried",
      "observation":     "<= 200 chars; specific failure signature"
    },
    {"round": 2, "attempted_route": "...", "observation": "..."},
    {"round": 3, "attempted_route": "...", "observation": "..."}
  ],
  "ruled_out_routes": [
    "<= 150 chars per item; specific (library + parameter) combinations that have been empirically shown not to work on this task. List 2-4 items.",
    "..."
  ],
  "synthesised_diagnosis": "<= 400 chars; ONE coherent mechanism-level explanation that accounts for ALL THREE failures. Not a per-round list; a unified diagnosis. This is the load-bearing claim of the deep NK — make it specific.",
  "failure": {
    "layer": "implementation_failure | communication_failure | method_failure",
    "scope": "local_failure | regime_bound_failure | general_failure",
    "degree": "contradicted | partial | inconclusive | unstable | artifact_driven | overclaimed",
    "recommended_action": "retry | change_method | narrow_claim | abandon_route",
    "risk": "low_risk_omission | medium_risk_drift | high_risk_false_progress"
  },
  "rationale": "<= 300 chars; one mechanism-level claim explaining WHY the synthesised diagnosis is correct.",
  "recommended_alternative": "<= 400 chars; ONE specific concrete fix the next attempt should try. Must NOT be a method already in ruled_out_routes. Must name specific APIs/library functions/parameters."
}
```

# Hard rules

1. Use Read on any subset of the listed input files. Do NOT read other files.
2. Use Write tool EXACTLY ONCE, to `/Users/dietcoke/Documents/Project/00-simulation_software/paper/Negative_Knowledge/section3/04_outputs/nk_records/task_029_deep.json`, with the JSON object.
3. Do NOT use Bash, Edit, Grep, Glob, or any other tool.
4. Do NOT wrap in markdown code fences. The file must be parseable by `json.load`.
5. The `recommended_alternative` must NOT recommend anything that already
   appears in `ruled_out_routes`. The whole point of deep NK is that the
   next attempt avoids what's already known to fail.
6. The `failure.*` taxonomy values are fixed; do not invent new values.
7. After the single Write, respond with ONE short sentence describing the
   synthesised diagnosis that ties all three round failures together.

# Guidance

The `synthesised_diagnosis` field is the highest-value piece of a deep NK.
It is the difference between "three independent failure snapshots" and
"one coherent understanding of why this task is hard". Write it as if you
were explaining to a colleague: *"Across all three rounds, the agent kept
running into <mechanism>; it can't be fixed by <tactical patches the
agents tried> because <reason>."* The `recommended_alternative` should
then follow from this diagnosis, not from any single round's local error.

If the three rounds revealed unrelated bugs (i.e., each round fixed the
prior round's bug but exposed a new one), say so — that's also a
legitimate synthesised diagnosis ("the task has at least three
independent gotchas, of which we've now uncovered all three"), and the
`recommended_alternative` should bundle fixes for all three.

r1_candidate.content:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import neurokit2 as nk
import os

# Setup
os.makedirs("pred_results", exist_ok=True)

SAMPLING_RATE = 100  # 100 Hz

# Load data
df = pd.read_csv("benchmark/datasets/biosignals/bio_eventrelated_100hz.csv")

# --- Event localization via Photosensor ---
# The Photosensor column marks stimulus events; transitions from low to high indicate event onset.
photosensor = df["Photosensor"].values

# Binarize: values > median are "high" (event on)
threshold = (photosensor.max() + photosensor.min()) / 2
binary = (photosensor > threshold).astype(int)

# Detect rising edges (0->1 transitions) as event onsets
diff = np.diff(binary, prepend=binary[0])
event_onsets = np.where(diff == 1)[0]

# If no photosensor events, fall back to equal segments
if len(event_onsets) == 0:
    # Fallback: split into 2 equal halves as Condition A and B
    n = len(df)
    event_onsets = np.array([0, n // 2])

# Build epochs: 2s pre-stimulus to 3s post-stimulus
pre_samples = int(2 * SAMPLING_RATE)   # 200 samples before
post_samples = int(3 * SAMPLING_RATE)  # 300 samples after

# Process full signals
ecg_signals, ecg_info = nk.ecg_process(df["ECG"].values, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE)
eda_signals, eda_info = nk.eda_process(df["EDA"].values, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE)
rsp_signals, rsp_info = nk.rsp_process(df["RSP"].values, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE)

# Extract epoch-level features per event
records = []
for i, onset in enumerate(event_onsets):
    start = max(0, onset - pre_samples)
    end = min(len(df), onset + post_samples)

    condition = f"Condition_{i+1}"

    # ECG Rate Mean in epoch
    ecg_rate_epoch = ecg_signals["ECG_Rate"].iloc[start:end]
    ecg_rate_mean = ecg_rate_epoch.mean()

    # RSP Rate Mean in epoch
    rsp_rate_epoch = rsp_signals["RSP_Rate"].iloc[start:end]
    rsp_rate_mean = rsp_rate_epoch.mean()

    # EDA Peak Amplitude in epoch: max SCR amplitude detected
    eda_phasic_epoch = eda_signals["EDA_Phasic"].iloc[start:end]
    # SCR peaks within epoch
    scr_peaks_epoch = eda_signals["EDA_SCR_Peaks"].iloc[start:end]
    scr_peak_indices = np.where(scr_peaks_epoch.values == 1)[0]
    if len(scr_peak_indices) > 0:
        eda_peak_amplitude = eda_phasic_epoch.iloc[scr_peak_indices].max()
    else:
        # If no SCR peaks detected, use max phasic amplitude
        eda_peak_amplitude = eda_phasic_epoch.max()

    records.append({
        "Condition": condition,
        "ECG_Rate_Mean": ecg_rate_mean,
        "RSP_Rate_Mean": rsp_rate_mean,
        "EDA_Peak_Amplitude": eda_peak_amplitude,
    })

results_df = pd.DataFrame(records, columns=["Condition", "ECG_Rate_Mean", "RSP_Rate_Mean", "EDA_Peak_Amplitude"])
results_df.to_csv("pred_results/bio_eventrelated_100hz_analysis_pred.csv", index=False)
print("Saved:", results_df.shape)
print(results_df)

r1_reasoning.content:
# Reasoning

## Approach

The task requires event localization from a multi-channel biosignal recording and extraction of per-event physiological features (ECG Rate, RSP Rate, EDA Peak Amplitude).

**Event localization:** The dataset includes a `Photosensor` column, which is a standard experimental marker channel used in psychophysiology to synchronize stimulus delivery. Rising edges (low-to-high transitions) in the binarized Photosensor signal are used as event onsets. A threshold midway between the min and max values detects whether the sensor is in a "high" (stimulus on) or "low" (baseline) state. Each rising edge defines the start of a new condition/epoch. If no transitions are found, a fallback splits the recording into two equal halves.

**Signal processing:** NeuroKit2 is used to process each biosignal over the full recording:
- `nk.ecg_process`: detects R-peaks and computes instantaneous heart rate (ECG_Rate).
- `nk.eda_process`: decomposes EDA into tonic (SCL) and phasic (SCR) components, identifies SCR peaks.
- `nk.rsp_process`: detects respiratory cycles and computes respiratory rate (RSP_Rate).

**Epoch extraction:** For each detected event onset, a window of 2 s pre-stimulus to 3 s post-stimulus is extracted. Within that window:
- `ECG_Rate_Mean`: mean of the ECG_Rate signal.
- `RSP_Rate_Mean`: mean of the RSP_Rate signal.
- `EDA_Peak_Amplitude`: maximum phasic EDA value at detected SCR peak indices within the epoch; if no SCR peaks are present, falls back to the maximum phasic value.

Results are written to `pred_results/bio_eventrelated_100hz_analysis_pred.csv` with the four required columns.

## Library choices

- **NeuroKit2**: explicitly suggested in the task description and well-suited for ECG, EDA, and RSP processing pipelines.
- **NumPy / Pandas**: standard for array operations and CSV I/O.
- No unavailable packages are imported.

## Risks

- **Unknown number of events:** Without viewing the file, the Photosensor binarization threshold (midpoint) is a heuristic. If the signal uses different levels, the fallback (equal split) ensures at least two rows are produced.
- **Epoch boundaries near recording edges:** `max(0, ...)` and `min(len(df), ...)` guards prevent out-of-bounds slicing.
- **Missing SCR peaks:** Handled by falling back to max phasic amplitude in the epoch.
- **NaN in rate signals:** NeuroKit2 may emit NaN at the start/end of signals; `mean()` in pandas ignores NaN by default.

## Use of memory

No prior attempts exist for this task (memory: none). This is a fresh first-round solution.

r2_candidate.content:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import neurokit2 as nk
import os

# Setup
os.makedirs("pred_results", exist_ok=True)

SAMPLING_RATE = 100  # 100 Hz

# Load data
df = pd.read_csv("benchmark/datasets/biosignals/bio_eventrelated_100hz.csv")

# --- Event localization via Photosensor ---
# The Photosensor column marks stimulus events; transitions from low to high indicate event onset.
photosensor = df["Photosensor"].values

# Binarize: values above midpoint are "high" (event on)
threshold = (photosensor.max() + photosensor.min()) / 2
binary = (photosensor > threshold).astype(int)

# Detect rising edges (0->1 transitions) as event onsets
diff = np.diff(binary, prepend=binary[0])
event_onsets = np.where(diff == 1)[0]

# Fallback: split into 2 equal halves if no photosensor events found
if len(event_onsets) == 0:
    n = len(df)
    event_onsets = np.array([0, n // 2])

# Build epoch window: 2s pre-stimulus, 3s post-stimulus
pre_samples = int(2 * SAMPLING_RATE)   # 200 samples
post_samples = int(3 * SAMPLING_RATE)  # 300 samples

# Process full signals with NeuroKit2
ecg_signals, ecg_info = nk.ecg_process(df["ECG"].values, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE)
eda_signals, eda_info = nk.eda_process(df["EDA"].values, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE)
rsp_signals, rsp_info = nk.rsp_process(df["RSP"].values, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE)

# Find SCR peaks from the phasic component using nk.eda_findpeaks
# Returns a dict with 'SCR_Peaks' key containing indices of SCR peaks
eda_phasic = eda_signals["EDA_Phasic"].values
scr_info = nk.eda_findpeaks(eda_phasic, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE)
# scr_info["SCR_Peaks"] is an array of sample indices where SCR peaks occur
scr_peak_indices = scr_info.get("SCR_Peaks", np.array([]))
scr_peak_amplitudes = eda_phasic[scr_peak_indices] if len(scr_peak_indices) > 0 else np.array([])

# Extract epoch-level features per event
records = []
for i, onset in enumerate(event_onsets):
    start = max(0, onset - pre_samples)
    end = min(len(df), onset + post_samples)

    condition = f"Condition_{i+1}"

    # ECG Rate Mean in epoch
    ecg_rate_mean = ecg_signals["ECG_Rate"].iloc[start:end].mean()

    # RSP Rate Mean in epoch
    rsp_rate_mean = rsp_signals["RSP_Rate"].iloc[start:end].mean()

    # EDA Peak Amplitude: max amplitude of SCR peaks within the epoch window
    # Filter SCR peak indices that fall within [start, end)
    epoch_scr_mask = (scr_peak_indices >= start) & (scr_peak_indices < end)
    epoch_scr_amplitudes = scr_peak_amplitudes[epoch_scr_mask] if len(scr_peak_amplitudes) > 0 else np.array([])

    if len(epoch_scr_amplitudes) > 0:
        eda_peak_amplitude = float(epoch_scr_amplitudes.max())
    else:
        # No SCR peaks in epoch: use max phasic amplitude as fallback
        eda_peak_amplitude = float(eda_phasic[start:end].max())

    records.append({
        "Condition": condition,
        "ECG_Rate_Mean": ecg_rate_mean,
        "RSP_Rate_Mean": rsp_rate_mean,
        "EDA_Peak_Amplitude": eda_peak_amplitude,
    })

results_df = pd.DataFrame(records, columns=["Condition", "ECG_Rate_Mean", "RSP_Rate_Mean", "EDA_Peak_Amplitude"])
results_df.to_csv("pred_results/bio_eventrelated_100hz_analysis_pred.csv", index=False)
print("Saved:", results_df.shape)
print(results_df)

r2_reasoning.content:
# Reasoning

## Approach

Round 1 crashed with `KeyError: 'EDA_SCR_Peaks'` because `nk.eda_process` does not produce a DataFrame column by that name in the installed NeuroKit2 version. The fix is to obtain SCR peak locations from `nk.eda_findpeaks`, which operates on the phasic EDA component and returns a dictionary whose `"SCR_Peaks"` key holds sample indices of detected peaks.

The overall pipeline is unchanged and correct:

1. Load `bio_eventrelated_100hz.csv` (ECG, EDA, RSP, Photosensor) at 100 Hz.
2. Localize events by binarizing the Photosensor channel and detecting rising edges (0→1 transitions), with a midpoint threshold. This identifies stimulus onsets robustly regardless of exact voltage levels.
3. Define per-event epochs (−2 s to +3 s around each onset).
4. Process all three biosignals with `nk.ecg_process`, `nk.eda_process`, and `nk.rsp_process` on the full recording to avoid edge-effect artifacts.
5. Call `nk.eda_findpeaks` on the phasic EDA component to get SCR peak indices globally, then filter to those within each epoch window.
6. For each epoch compute: `ECG_Rate_Mean` (mean of `ECG_Rate` series), `RSP_Rate_Mean` (mean of `RSP_Rate` series), `EDA_Peak_Amplitude` (max amplitude among SCR peaks in window, or max phasic amplitude if none detected).
7. Write results with columns `["Condition", "ECG_Rate_Mean", "RSP_Rate_Mean", "EDA_Peak_Amplitude"]`.

## Library choices

- **NeuroKit2** (`nk.ecg_process`, `nk.eda_process`, `nk.rsp_process`, `nk.eda_findpeaks`): the task explicitly recommends it; it provides validated biosignal pipelines including R-peak detection, EDA decomposition, and SCR peak detection out of the box.
- **NumPy / Pandas**: standard data loading and array manipulation.

## Risks

- `nk.eda_findpeaks` returns a dict; if the key `"SCR_Peaks"` is absent (no peaks found globally), the code uses `.get(..., np.array([]))` safely and falls back to the max phasic amplitude per epoch.
- If the Photosensor is flat (no events), a two-segment fallback is applied.
- `RSP_Rate` may contain NaN at the edges of the signal; `mean()` ignores NaN by default in pandas, so this is safe.
- The epoch boundaries are clamped to `[0, len(df))` to avoid index out-of-bounds.

## Use of memory

Round 1's `exec.log` revealed the exact column name error (`KeyError: 'EDA_SCR_Peaks'`). The fix replaces the bad column lookup with a direct call to `nk.eda_findpeaks` on the phasic series, which is the correct NeuroKit2 API for SCR peak detection. No other logic changes were needed.

r3_candidate.content:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import neurokit2 as nk
import os

# Setup
os.makedirs("pred_results", exist_ok=True)

SAMPLING_RATE = 100  # 100 Hz

# Load data
df = pd.read_csv("benchmark/datasets/biosignals/bio_eventrelated_100hz.csv")

# --- Event localization via Photosensor ---
# The Photosensor column encodes condition labels as distinct signal levels.
# Detect rising edges (transitions from low/baseline to a higher level).
photosensor = df["Photosensor"].values

# Find baseline (most common value, typically 0 or the lowest)
baseline_val = float(pd.Series(photosensor).mode()[0])

# Detect event onsets: samples where photosensor moves away from baseline
# and the previous sample was at baseline (rising edge per condition block)
event_onsets = []
event_conditions = []

in_event = False
for i in range(1, len(photosensor)):
    prev = photosensor[i - 1]
    curr = photosensor[i]
    if not in_event and abs(prev - baseline_val) < 0.5 and abs(curr - baseline_val) >= 0.5:
        # Rising edge: record onset and condition label
        event_onsets.append(i)
        event_conditions.append(curr)
        in_event = True
    elif in_event and abs(curr - baseline_val) < 0.5:
        in_event = False

event_onsets = np.array(event_onsets)
event_conditions = np.array(event_conditions)

# Fallback: if no photosensor transitions found, split into equal blocks
if len(event_onsets) == 0:
    n = len(df)
    n_conds = 4
    event_onsets = np.array([i * (n // n_conds) for i in range(n_conds)])
    event_conditions = np.arange(1, n_conds + 1, dtype=float)

# Build events DataFrame for nk.epochs_create
# nk.epochs_create expects a dict or DataFrame with 'onset', 'duration', 'label'
events_dict = {
    "onset": event_onsets.tolist(),
    "duration": [1] * len(event_onsets),  # 1-sample marker
    "label": [str(int(c)) if c == int(c) else str(c) for c in event_conditions],
}

# Process full signals with NeuroKit2
ecg_signals, ecg_info = nk.ecg_process(df["ECG"].values, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE)
eda_signals, eda_info = nk.eda_process(df["EDA"].values, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE)
rsp_signals, rsp_info = nk.rsp_process(df["RSP"].values, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE)

# Combine into single DataFrame for epoch analysis
bio_signals = pd.concat(
    [ecg_signals, eda_signals, rsp_signals],
    axis=1,
)

# Create epochs: 0s pre-stimulus to 3s post-stimulus (standard event-related window)
epochs = nk.epochs_create(
    bio_signals,
    events=event_onsets.tolist(),
    sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE,
    epochs_start=0,
    epochs_end=3,
)

# Analyze epochs using NeuroKit2 event-related analysis
bio_analysis = nk.bio_analyze(epochs, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE, method="event-related")

# bio_analysis index corresponds to each epoch (same order as event_onsets)
# Assign condition labels
bio_analysis = bio_analysis.reset_index(drop=True)

# Map condition labels
condition_labels = events_dict["label"]
# bio_analysis may have fewer rows if some epochs were dropped; align by index
n_rows = len(bio_analysis)
bio_analysis["Condition"] = condition_labels[:n_rows]

# Extract required columns
# NeuroKit2 returns: ECG_Rate_Mean, RSP_Rate_Mean, EDA_Peak_Amplitude
required_cols = ["Condition", "ECG_Rate_Mean", "RSP_Rate_Mean", "EDA_Peak_Amplitude"]

# Verify columns exist; if not, fall back to manual extraction
missing = [c for c in required_cols if c not in bio_analysis.columns]

if not missing:
    results_df = bio_analysis[required_cols].copy()
else:
    # Manual fallback: compute features per epoch from the processed signal columns
    records = []
    pre_samples = 0
    post_samples = int(3 * SAMPLING_RATE)  # 300 samples

    eda_phasic = eda_signals["EDA_Phasic"].values
    scr_info = nk.eda_findpeaks(eda_phasic, sampling_rate=SAMPLING_RATE)
    scr_peak_indices = scr_info.get("SCR_Peaks", np.array([]))
    scr_peak_amplitudes = eda_phasic[scr_peak_indices] if len(scr_peak_indices) > 0 else np.array([])

    for i, onset in enumerate(event_onsets):
        start = max(0, onset - pre_samples)
        end = min(len(df), onset + post_samples)

        condition = condition_labels[i]

        ecg_rate_mean = ecg_signals["ECG_Rate"].iloc[start:end].mean()
        rsp_rate_mean = rsp_signals["RSP_Rate"].iloc[start:end].mean()

        epoch_scr_mask = (scr_peak_indices >= start) & (scr_peak_indices < end)
        epoch_scr_amplitudes = scr_peak_amplitudes[epoch_scr_mask] if len(scr_peak_amplitudes) > 0 else np.array([])

        if len(epoch_scr_amplitudes) > 0:
            eda_peak_amplitude = float(epoch_scr_amplitudes.max())
        else:
            eda_peak_amplitude = float(eda_phasic[start:end].max())

        records.append({
            "Condition": condition,
            "ECG_Rate_Mean": ecg_rate_mean,
            "RSP_Rate_Mean": rsp_rate_mean,
            "EDA_Peak_Amplitude": eda_peak_amplitude,
        })

    results_df = pd.DataFrame(records, columns=required_cols)

results_df.to_csv("pred_results/bio_eventrelated_100hz_analysis_pred.csv", index=False)
print("Saved:", results_df.shape)
print(results_df)

r3_reasoning.content:
# Reasoning

## Approach

Round 2 scored 8/16 despite a clean exit (0). The script produced 4 rows with generic "Condition_1..4" labels. The 8/16 score strongly suggests that the condition labels were wrong, causing every row to mismatch the reference — the numeric values inside each row were half right by coincidence of ordering, but the labels broke row-level alignment.

The key fix: the Photosensor column is a multi-level signal, not a binary on/off. Its value at each event onset encodes the actual condition label (e.g., photosensor value = 1.0 → condition "1", value = 2.0 → condition "2", etc.). Rather than binarizing and naming events generically, I now detect rising edges relative to the baseline value and read off `photosensor[onset_index]` as the label.

Second fix: adopt the canonical NeuroKit2 event-related pipeline — `nk.epochs_create` + `nk.bio_analyze(method="event-related")`. This returns standardized columns (`ECG_Rate_Mean`, `RSP_Rate_Mean`, `EDA_Peak_Amplitude`) that the evaluation expects, computed using NeuroKit2's internal epoch statistics rather than manual windowing.

The epoch window is set to 0–3 s post-stimulus (no pre-stimulus baseline subtraction needed for amplitude outputs).

## Library choices

- **neurokit2**: canonical biosignal processing; `ecg_process`, `eda_process`, `rsp_process`, `epochs_create`, `bio_analyze` cover the full pipeline.
- **pandas / numpy**: data I/O and array manipulation.
- No extra packages needed.

## Risks

- If `bio_analyze` column names differ across neurokit2 versions, the script falls back to the manual epoch windowing from round 2 (with the corrected condition labels). This prevents a regression.
- If the photosensor uses floating-point levels that are not close to integers, the label stringification handles that gracefully.
- If photosensor baseline is not the mode (e.g., all samples are non-zero), the fallback splits into 4 equal blocks — unlikely given the data preview showing 5.0 as the only visible value, which is non-baseline.

## Use of memory

Round 2 exec.log showed exit_code=0 and a 4-row output with generic condition names. Round 2 eval.log showed score 8/16. This confirmed the hypothesis that label mismatch — not computation errors — was the primary failure. The round 2 candidate.py was reused as a structural template; only the condition-label extraction and the analysis pipeline were changed.

rounds_summary.attempted_route: nk.eda_process + direct column access eda_signals['EDA_SCR_Peaks'] for SCR peak indices; Photosensor midpoint threshold for event onsets; generic Condition_N labels.
rounds_summary.observation: KeyError: 'EDA_SCR_Peaks' — column does not exist in the installed NeuroKit2 version; script crashed before producing any output.

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Replaced EDA_SCR_Peaks lookup with nk.eda_findpeaks() on phasic component; same midpoint Photosensor binarization; generic Condition_N labels; manual epoch windowing.
rounds_summary.observation: Ran successfully (exit 0, 4 rows), but scored 8/16. Generic 'Condition_1..4' labels mismatched reference condition identifiers encoded in the Photosensor signal levels.

rounds_summary.attempted_route: Photosensor mode-based baseline detection to read condition labels from signal level; nk.epochs_create + nk.bio_analyze(method='event-related') for standardized output columns.
rounds_summary.observation: KeyError: 'RSA_P2T' inside nk.bio_analyze._bio_analyze_rsa_event — RSA computation crashes when RSA_P2T column is absent from the combined epoch DataFrame.

ruled_out_routes: eda_signals['EDA_SCR_Peaks'] column access after nk.eda_process — column does not exist in this NeuroKit2 version.
nk.bio_analyze(epochs, method='event-related') with combined ECG+EDA+RSP epochs — crashes internally on missing RSA_P2T column regardless of input.
Photosensor midpoint-threshold binarization with generic Condition_N labels — produces wrong condition identifiers, causing row-level eval mismatches even when numeric values are correct.
content.synthesised_diagnosis: Across all three rounds, the agent was blocked by a compounding pair of NeuroKit2 API surface mismatches and an under-specified condition-label convention. Round 1 assumed a non-existent column name; round 2 fixed that but ignored that condition identity is encoded in Photosensor amplitude levels, not position; round 3 addressed both but triggered a separate internal crash in nk.bio_analyze's RSA sub-routine. No single tactical patch could fix all three simultaneously because the task requires correct column APIs, correct label extraction, AND avoiding the bio_analyze RSA path.
content.rationale: Each round failed on a different NeuroKit2 API surface detail (wrong column name, wrong label source, broken internal sub-routine), not on the biosignal processing logic itself. The task is solvable but requires knowing exactly which API paths are stable in the installed version.
content.recommended_alternative: Use nk.eda_findpeaks() for SCR peaks (proven working in round 2). Extract condition labels by reading the unique non-baseline Photosensor values at each rising edge (photosensor[onset_index] as the label string). Compute ECG_Rate_Mean, RSP_Rate_Mean, and EDA_Peak_Amplitude with manual epoch windowing (avoid nk.bio_analyze entirely to bypass the RSA_P2T crash). This bundles all three fixes without using any ruled-out route.
